skill.name: golang-testing
skill.description: Guidance for writing Go tests, including table-driven tests, mocks, integration tests, benchmarks, fixtures, and coverage workflows.
skill.summary: Build structured, maintainable Go test suites with common patterns and examples.
skill.author: Joseph OBrien

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged dynamic imports, backtick execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Manual review found these are markdown examples, Go import blocks, prose, or test identifiers rather than executable skill code. The only retained risk factor is benign external command guidance for Go test and coverage commands.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Go Import Blocks Flagged as Dynamic Imports
low_findings.description: The flagged locations are Go import blocks inside markdown examples. They do not use JavaScript dynamic import expressions and are not executed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain static Go import declarations in fenced examples. No dynamic loader or runtime import call is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The flagged backticks are markdown code fences and inline command examples for Go testing. The skill contains documentation only and does not run these commands automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All locations are fenced code blocks, inline markdown, or instructional examples. No script file or execution wrapper is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Signatures
low_findings.description: The flagged locations are descriptive text, sample test data, function names, context variables, and test naming advice. No cryptographic algorithm use or host reconnaissance behavior appears in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found only benign Go testing examples at these lines. There are no hash functions, system inventory commands, or data collection routines.

content.value_statement: Go projects need tests that are clear, fast, and reliable. This skill provides practical patterns for unit tests, mocks, integration tests, benchmarks, and coverage checks.
actual_capabilities: Shows table-driven test structures for normal cases and error cases.
Explains interface-based mocks for repositories, services, and senders.
Provides integration test examples using Testcontainers and PostgreSQL.
Outlines fixture setup, teardown, and per-test cleanup patterns.
Demonstrates benchmark, sub-benchmark, and parallel benchmark layouts.
Documents coverage commands and test organization conventions.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, not a runnable test generator by itself.
Examples use simplified domain types that must be adapted to each project.
Integration test examples require Docker and Go dependencies to be installed.
It does not replace project-specific review of flaky tests or race conditions.

use_cases.title: Add Unit Tests to a Go Package
use_cases.description: Create table-driven tests that cover success paths, failures, boundaries, and nil inputs.

use_cases.title: Test Service Code with Mocks
use_cases.description: Use interfaces and small mock implementations to isolate repositories, services, and external senders.

use_cases.title: Improve CI Test Coverage
use_cases.description: Organize fast tests, integration tests, benchmarks, and coverage commands for continuous integration.

prompt_templates.title: Write a Basic Go Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the golang-testing skill to write table-driven tests for this Go function. Include success cases, edge cases, and clear failure messages.

prompt_templates.title: Add Error Case Coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the golang-testing skill to expand these tests with expected errors, nil inputs, boundary values, and readable subtest names.

prompt_templates.title: Mock Go Dependencies
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the golang-testing skill to design interface-based mocks for this service. Show how each dependency should be controlled in tests.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Full Go Test Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the golang-testing skill to propose a test strategy for this Go repository. Include unit tests, integration tests, benchmarks, fixtures, and CI commands.

output_examples.input: A Go function returns values and errors for several input combinations.
output_examples.output: A table-driven test plan with named cases, expected results, expected errors, and focused assertions.

output_examples.input: A service depends on a user repository and an email sender.
output_examples.output: A mock-based testing approach that defines interfaces, injects dependencies, and verifies success and failure behavior.

output_examples.input: A repository needs database integration tests and coverage reporting.
output_examples.output: A test organization plan with integration build tags, container setup, cleanup steps, and coverage commands.

best_practices: Use descriptive subtest names that explain the behavior under test.
Keep unit tests fast and isolate slow integration tests with build tags or short mode.
Clean up databases, files, and other shared resources after each test.
anti_patterns: Do not test private implementation details when public behavior is enough.
Do not share mutable fixtures across tests without clear cleanup.
Do not rely on integration tests for behavior that unit tests can cover quickly.

faq.question: Does this skill generate complete Go test files?
faq.answer: It provides patterns and examples that an AI assistant can adapt into complete test files.

faq.question: Can it help with table-driven tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes examples for named cases, expected values, expected errors, and subtests.

faq.question: Does it support mocking guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It focuses on interface-based mocks that keep tests simple and explicit.

faq.question: Can it help with integration tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a Testcontainers pattern for PostgreSQL-backed repository tests.

faq.question: Does it run commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It documents Go test and coverage commands for the user or assistant to run intentionally.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: It is listed for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
